hypothesis: Combining VORTEX with Bollinger Bands creates a robust mean-reversion signal by identifying momentum shifts in trending markets, while using ADX as a trend filter prevents false signals during strong trends. The ATR-based risk management ensures dynamic stops that adapt to volatility conditions.

Combining EMA with Bollinger bands and VORTEX creates a robust mean-reversion signal that filters out trend-based moves. When price is below the lower Bollinger band and EMA crosses above it, it suggests a potential reversal. VORTEX rising from a local minimum confirms the strength of the reversal signal, while ATR-based stop-loss ensures proper risk management in varying volatility conditions.

Combining EMA crossover with Bollinger band reversion and VORTEX trend confirmation creates a robust mean-reversion signal that filters out noise during ranging markets while capturing strong momentum reversals. The VORTEX indicator ensures entries occur only when the market shows clear directional strength, reducing false signals. ATR-based risk management adapts to volatility conditions for optimal position sizing.

Combining VORTEX as a trend strength indicator with Bollinger Bands for mean-reversion signals creates a robust filter that avoids trading against the trend. When VORTEX is rising, it confirms the market is in a trending regime, making mean-reversion strategies more reliable. The Bollinger crossover provides clear entry points, while ATR-based risk management ensures dynamic stop-losses that adapt to volatility.

Combining VORTEX as a trend strength indicator with Bollinger Bands for mean-reversion signals creates a robust filtering mechanism that avoids trading against strong trends while capitalizing on range-bound market conditions. The VORTEX oscillator helps identify when the market is transitioning from trend to range, making entries more reliable.
